The '''imperative''' mood is the most common mood in Aelaki, used extensively in everyday speech. Approximately 80% of first-person future statements employ the imperative rather than the standard indicative.

== Overview ==

The imperative mood originally applied only to transitive verbs, where it was marked by a shift to SOV word order. Over time, it expanded to cover intransitive verbs as well, using the dummy pronoun ''üfüf'' (or ''ïfïf'') to fill the object slot.

When used with a first-person subject, the imperative carries the force of a '''promise''' or firm commitment rather than a command.

== Template ==

The modern imperative template is:

: <code>[SUBJ-Ki][OBJ-Ki]-VERB</code>

Both the subject and object take the ''-Ki'' case marker, followed by the verb stem.

== Example ==

: '''Thu-bal-zar''' — "Let me catch two (fish)!"

In this example, the first-person subject makes a promise to catch two fish, demonstrating the promissory force of the first-person imperative.

== Historical Development ==

The imperative originally arose from a word order alternation in transitive clauses. The standard word order shifted to SOV to signal imperative force. Intransitive verbs later developed their own imperative construction by introducing the dummy pronoun ''üfüf''/''ïfïf'' to satisfy the template's requirement for an object slot.

[[Category:evidentialities]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
